name: PennyLane
description: A quantum machine-learning and hybrid-algorithm framework built around differentiable quantum circuits.
whyUseIt: PennyLane is especially strong when you want to connect quantum circuits to optimisation loops, gradient-based training, or machine-learning-style workflows.
fieldPosition: Hybrid algorithms, variational methods, and differentiable quantum programming
engineerWorkflow: Define a parameterised circuit, choose a device backend, embed it in an optimisation loop, and inspect how the objective evolves.
managerExpects: Hybrid workflows that are justified by the problem rather than chosen because they sound modern.
demoDescription: Build a simple VQE loop and inspect how parameter updates change the estimated energy.
beginnerMistakes: Using variational methods without a clear objective or baseline
Confusing a working optimisation loop with proof of quantum advantage
